sourceMovie.title: Eastern Promises
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eastern Promises (2007) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Cronenberg's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Collateral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eastern Promises clicked for you, Collateral (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eastern Promises?
faq.answer: Try Collateral, The Talented Mr. Ripley, Secret Window—each shares DNA with Eastern Promises while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eastern Promises?
faq.answer: Eastern Promises (2007) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eastern Promises worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
